name: Aretha Franklin
abstract: Aretha Louise Franklin ( ə-REE-thə; March 25, 1942 – August 16, 2018) was an American singer, songwriter and pianist. Regarded as the "Queen of Soul", she was twice named by Rolling Stone magazine as the greatest singer of all time.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Aretha Franklin, Singer

works_created.t: Dr. Feelgood (Love Is a Serious Business)
(Sweet Sweet Baby) Since You've Been Gone

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: Aretha Franklin doing “A Natural Woman,” you know, or Al Green doing “Let’s Stay Together,” okay or Jimi Hendrix doing “Hey Joe,” the emotional response is still the same.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: if I woke up in Switzerland and I heard somebody yodeling everyday, it ain't like hearing B.B. King or Aretha Franklin or Miles Davis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Byron Stripling
about_quotes.evidence: Look at Aretha Franklin was raised in the church through the way she sang, you know — was able to really get her talent going there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Byron Stripling interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 25, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Shaffer
about_quotes.evidence: Once I knocked her off and got to play for her a number of times, even produce her doing “Respect” for the Blues Brothers sequel
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Shaffer interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: the years spent with Aretha... I had more problems than a barrel of monkeys on the personal side but that’s what I had to learn to live with. Aretha is one of the best, one of the best performers in the world.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: He was, you know, he was the guy — I mean he discovered Bruce Springsteen and George Benson and Aretha Franklin and all these people. I mean he brought them to fame.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019
